mechanism_narrative: Steady increases in consumer prices signal gradual demand expansion that supports nominal corporate revenue growth, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Twenty-day forward returns following month-over-month CPIAUCSL increases are not positive in greater than 55 percent of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above its 12-month minimum by more than 0.3 percentage points signals weakening labor income and impending demand contraction, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 50 percent of triggered instances within any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Jointly rising consumer prices and a declining federal funds rate produce a negative real interest rate environment that stimulates risk assets, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint signal Rank IC falls below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or the signal underperforms the unconditional baseline by less than 2 percent annualized.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, month-over-month declines in industrial production signal margin compression under elevated discount rates, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.5% and INDPRO declines, S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in fewer than 50 percent of instances over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real retail sales signals robust final demand that drives earnings expectations higher, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Sixty-day forward returns following positive RSAFS momentum are not significantly greater than zero at the 5 percent level over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 3.5% restricts liquidity and increases debt-service costs, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are negative in fewer than 55 percent of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 3.5% over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts reflect strong construction activity and household confidence, which spill over into broad equity performance, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in HOUST do not coincide with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55 percent of occurrences over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment has fallen for two consecutive months, the cumulative loss in labor income triggers a demand shock that produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns following two consecutive monthly declines in PAYEMS are negative in fewer than 55 percent of occurrences over any 3-year rolling window.
